label: LLM-as-judge & automated critique

node.label: LLM-as-judge & automated critique
node.definition: LLM-as-judge uses a model to score, compare, critique, or classify outputs under a rubric, often calibrated against human labels and bias checks.
node.why_it_matters: The role of LLM-as-judge & automated critique is to connect Decoder-only causal transformers, Human evaluation & preference studies to downstream capabilities including Observability, tracing & evaluation harnesses.
examples: substrate for Observability, tracing & evaluation harnesses
limitations: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Decoder-only causal transformers, Human evaluation & preference studies.

dependencies.rationale: LLM-as-judge & automated critique measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Human evaluation & preference studies.
LLM-as-judge & automated critique measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Benchmarks, test suites & task metrics.
LLM-as-judge systems require a broad transformer-based judge model, not a system-layer LLM aggregate.
